title: [InstCombine] Fold `lshr -> zext -> shl` patterns
summary: The developers generalized the pattern by shifting from a hardcoded constant check to a bitmask-driven approach. Instead of testing a single shift amount, they compute a mask of all possible shift amounts that can be simplified (`getEvaluableShiftedShiftMask`). This mask is refined recursively (`refineEvaluableShiftMask`) across the expression tree, allowing the transformation to handle varied shift-and-mask combinations. The key insight is to use known-bit analysis to determine which outer shift amounts are safe to fold, enabling the optimization to apply to both masked and simple cases. This approach makes the transformation reusable and extensible, as it decouples the logic from specific constant values and leverages generic bit-tracking.
